text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
One day, while I'm strolling around the shopping
district...

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Great. What a mess I've gotten myself into... Let's
see... There's shrimp, mixed tempura, squid, bell
peppers...

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Oh, it's nothing serious. One of the older
shopkeepers in the shopping district gave me a
hefty amount of tempura.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
And while I'm incredibly happy and grateful for
his gift, there's simply no way I can finish it all.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Deep-fried foods are so oily and heavy, and
eating so much in one sitting is guaranteed to end
in a nasty bout of indigestion!
choice_data_list:
Eat it with grated daikon radish to make things
easier!
You should hold a tempura party at the dorm!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text: Grated daikon radish? How would that help?

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Interesting... That sounds just like the kind of
thing a grandmother would say, and I love golden
nuggets of wisdom like that.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Speaking of which, I remember my aunt teaching
me a good way to use up leftover tempura a long
time ago.

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
Okay, time to buy some daikon radishes at the
supermarket! I'll be able to eat as much tempura
as I want with their help!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
At the dorm? Oh, that's a great idea. I can just
leave it in the cafeteria!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
That way, I don't have to go around telling
everyone about the tempura, and anyone who
wants some can help themselves!

text_block_list.name: Nice Nature
text_block_list.text:
It'll be great exposure for the tasty tempura place
in the shopping district too. Yup, all's well that
ends well.
